ENGAA-2019-M1-002: The equation gives $y$ in terms of $x$: $y=3-4\left(1-\dfrac{x}{2}\right)^2$. Which one of the following is a rearrangement for $x$ in terms of $y$?
ENGAA-2019-M1-003: The diagram shows a right-angled triangle, with sides of length $x+4$, $2x+2$ and $3x$, all in cm (diagram not to scale). What is the area, in cm$^2$, of the triangle?
ENGAA-2019-M1-004: The resistance to the motion of a car is directly proportional to the square of the speed of the car. The car increases its speed by 20%. What is the percentage increase in the resistance to the motion of the car?
ENGAA-2019-M1-005: The equation of a curve is $y=px^2+qx$ where $p$ and $q$ are constants. The curve passes through the points $(2,6)$ and $(4,-4)$. What is the value of $q-p$?
ENGAA-2019-M1-006: Which of the following is a simplification of $4-\dfrac{x(3x+1)}{x^2(3x^2-2x-1)}$?
ENGAA-2019-M1-007: The ball for a garden game is a solid sphere of volume 192 cm$^3$. For the children's version of the game the ball is a solid sphere made of the same material, but the radius is reduced by 25%. What is the volume, in cm$^3$, of the children's ball?
ENGAA-2019-M1-008: $PR$ and $QS$ are the diagonals of a rhombus $PQRS$. $PR=(3x+2)$ cm, $QS=(8-2x)$ cm. The area of $PQRS$ is 11 cm$^2$. What is the difference, in cm, between the two possible lengths of $PR$?
ENGAA-2019-M1-009: $Q$, $S$, $P$, $R$, $T$: The diagram shows two congruent right-angled triangles $PQR$ and $TSR$ with right angles at $Q$ and $S$, respectively [DIAGRAM: two right triangles sharing vertex R, with PRT a straight line]. $PQ=TS=3$ cm, $QR=SR=4$ cm. $PRT$ is a straight line. What is the length, in cm, of $QS$?
ENGAA-2019-M1-010: A block is designed with a cylindrical channel to accommodate a hot-water pipe. The block is 100 cm long and has a square cross-section of side 22.0 cm with a cylindrical hole of diameter 14.0 cm through the middle (diagram not to scale). The density of the material is 0.100 g/cm$^3$. What is the mass of the block? (take $\pi = \frac{22}{7}$)
ENGAA-2019-M1-011: A solid pyramid has a square base of side length 12 cm and a vertical height of $h$ cm (diagram not to scale). The volume of the pyramid, in cm$^3$, is equal to the total surface area of the pyramid, in cm$^2$. What is the value of $h$? (volume of pyramid $=\frac13\times$ area of base $\times$ vertical height)
ENGAA-2019-PHY-019: A sample initially contains equal numbers of atoms of a radioactive isotope X and a stable isotope Y. Isotope X has a half-life of 3 years and decays in a single stage to the stable isotope Y. What is the ratio (number of atoms of X) : (number of atoms of Y) in the sample 6 years later?
ENGAA-2019-M2-001: Find the area of the shape bounded by the four lines: $2y+x=4$, $x=-6$, $x=0$, $y=0$.
ENGAA-2019-M2-002: The curve $y=x^3+px^2+qx+r$ has a local maximum when $x=-1$ and a local minimum when $x=3$. What is the value of $p$?
ENGAA-2019-M2-003: When simplified, $\dfrac{1}{(1-\sqrt2)^3}$ is written in the form $a+b\sqrt2$ where $a$ and $b$ are integers. What is the value of $b$?
ENGAA-2019-M2-004: It is given that $7\cos x+\tan x\sin x=5$ where $0°<x<90°$. What are the possible values of $\tan x$?
ENGAA-2019-M2-005: An equilateral triangle of side 8 cm is drawn so that its vertices lie on the circumference of a circle [DIAGRAM: equilateral triangle inscribed in a circle with three circular segments shaded]. What is the total of the three areas shaded in the diagram, in cm$^2$?
ENGAA-2019-M2-006: Which one of the following is the real solution of the equation $3\times5^{2x+1}-5^{x}-2=0$?
ENGAA-2019-M2-007: For a particular function $f(x)$, it is given that: $2\int_{-2}^{2}f(x)\,dx+\int_{2}^{4}f(x)\,dx=4$ and also: $5\int_{-2}^{2}f(x)\,dx-\int_{-2}^{4}f(x)\,dx=7$. Find the value of $\int_{2}^{4}f(x)\,dx$.
ENGAA-2019-M2-008: Given that $f(x)=\displaystyle\int_0^x (3+2t)^7\,dt$, what is the coefficient of $x^4$ in the expansion of $f(x)$ in powers of $x$?
ENGAA-2019-M2-009: The three internal angles in a triangle are $\alpha$, $\beta$ and $\theta$, and $3\tan\alpha-2\sin\beta=2$, $5\tan\alpha+6\sin\beta=8$. What is the value of $\theta$ in degrees?
ENGAA-2019-M2-010: Find the complete set of values of $x$ for which $x^3-2x^2-7x-4>0$.
ENGAA-2019-PHY-001: The current–voltage graph for a diode is shown [DIAGRAM: I-V characteristic curve for a diode, current (mA) 0-16 vs voltage (V) -0.6 to 1.4]. The diode is connected in series with a resistor and a 6.0 V battery. The current in the circuit is 8.0 mA. What is the resistance of the resistor? (Assume the battery has negligible resistance.)
ENGAA-2019-PHY-002: Two electromagnetic waves P and Q travel in a vacuum and the ratio $\dfrac{\text{wavelength of P}}{\text{wavelength of Q}}=1.0\times10^8$. Which row in the table shows the ratio of their speeds, the ratio of their frequencies, and identifies the possible natures of P and Q?
ENGAA-2019-PHY-003: A water-tight cylinder with a thin, freely moving piston contains $2.0\times10^{-3}\text{ m}^3$ of trapped air at atmospheric pressure of $1.0\times10^5$ Pa. When the cylinder is submerged in water of constant density 1000 kg m$^{-3}$, the volume of air decreases to $4.0\times10^{-4}\text{ m}^3$. The piston is at a depth $h$ below the surface. What is the depth $h$? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; temperature constant, air is ideal)
ENGAA-2019-PHY-004: The secondary coil of an ideal, 100% efficient transformer is connected to a resistor by cables of total resistance 1500 Ω. The current in the primary coil is 4.0 A. There are 240 turns in the primary coil and 4800 turns in the secondary coil. What is the power produced as heat in the cables?
ENGAA-2019-PHY-005: Two tanks of water are connected by a solid, insulated cylindrical copper bar of length $l$ and diameter $d$. One tank contains water at 90°C, the other at temperature $\theta$. For which of the following conditions is thermal energy conducted along the bar at the lowest rate?
ENGAA-2019-PHY-006: The radioactive isotope X becomes the stable isotope Y after a succession of decays involving only the emission of alpha and beta ($\beta^-$) particles. A total of seven particles are emitted, with more alpha particles than beta particles. The atomic number of X is Z and the mass number is A. Which row in the table could give the atomic number and mass number of Y?
ENGAA-2019-PHY-007: The kinetic energy of an object of mass 4.0 kg, travelling in a straight line, increases from 32 J to 200 J in 3.0 seconds due to a constant resultant force. What is the value of this resultant force?
ENGAA-2019-PHY-008: In the following circuit, all five resistors have the same resistance [DIAGRAM: circuit of 5 identical resistors with a voltmeter V connected across part of the network, and a battery]. The reading on the voltmeter is 1.0 V. What is the voltage across the battery?
ENGAA-2019-PHY-009: A hydroelectric power station uses water falling through a vertical height of 150 m to power turbines. The efficiency of the power station is 90% and the output power is 1800 MW. Given g=10 N kg$^{-1}$ and water density 1000 kg m$^{-3}$, what volume of water passes through the turbines in one minute?
ENGAA-2019-PHY-010: A car P of mass 1000 kg travelling north at 30 m/s hits car Q directly ahead, also travelling north, of mass 500 kg at 20 m/s. The collision lasts 0.20 s and immediately after, Q moves north at 30 m/s. What is the speed of P immediately after the collision and the average resultant force on Q during the collision? (Assume no external forces during the collision.)
ENGAA-2019-PHY-011: A metal wire of length 0.50 m has a uniform cross-sectional area of $4.0\times10^{-7}\text{ m}^2$. There is a current of 4.0 A in the wire. What is the potential difference across the ends of the wire? (resistivity of the metal = $1.6\times10^{-7}\ \Omega$m)
ENGAA-2019-PHY-012: A uniform square trap door of side 0.80 m and mass 14 kg has a smooth hinge at one edge and is held open at 30° to the horizontal. It is supported by a rigid rod meeting the door at 90°, 0.10 m from the top edge [DIAGRAM: trap door hinged at one edge, held at 30° by a perpendicular rod near the far edge]. What is the normal contact force exerted on the trap door by the rod? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$)
ENGAA-2019-PHY-013: Two small loudspeakers are placed side by side 30 cm apart, emitting sound of frequency 400 Hz in phase. A microphone is placed 40 cm directly in front of one loudspeaker [DIAGRAM: two speakers 30 cm apart, microphone 40 cm from one speaker forming a right triangle]. What is the phase difference between the waves from the loudspeakers as they arrive at the microphone? (speed of sound = 320 m/s)
ENGAA-2019-PHY-014: An astronaut on the Moon throws a ball vertically upwards. The ball has a mass of 2.0 g and is thrown upwards at 80 m/s. What is the maximum height gained by the ball? (g near the Moon's surface = 1.6 N kg$^{-1}$)
ENGAA-2019-PHY-015: A student has one 300 Ω resistor and another resistor of resistance R. The student plots a graph of current I against potential difference V for the 300 Ω resistor alone, and then for both resistors connected in parallel [DIAGRAM: I-V graph, two straight lines through the origin, I in mA 0-30 vs V in V 0-6]. What is the resistance R?
ENGAA-2019-PHY-016: A light, vertical, copper wire of length 2.4 m and uniform cross-sectional area $2.0\times10^{-6}\text{ m}^2$ supports a load of mass 4.0 kg. The Young modulus of copper is $1.2\times10^{11}$ Pa. What is the strain energy in the wire? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; wire obeys Hooke's law, cross-sectional area constant)
ENGAA-2019-PHY-017: Light travelling in a transparent liquid strikes the surface from below at angle $\alpha$ to the surface, and refracts to travel exactly along the surface [DIAGRAM: light ray from liquid grazing along the surface at angle α to the surface]. Separately, light travelling in air strikes the surface from above at the same angle $\alpha$ to the surface, refracting into the liquid at angle $\beta$ to the surface. Which expression gives $\beta$? (all angles in degrees)
